title: citrus medica f. aurantiifolium
intro: Citrus medica f. aurantiifolium, also known as bitter orange or Seville orange, is a variety of the sour orange tree (Citrus aurantium) that has been cultivated for its medicinal and culinary properties. The plant is prized for its essential oils, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and anxiety, due to their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and adaptogenic properties. The health benefits of Citrus medica f. aurantiifolium include reducing stress and anxiety, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, while also being used as a natural remedy for fever, colds, and flu. In herbal preparations, the plant's essential oils can be extracted through cold pressing or steam distillation, and are often used in aromatherapy, skincare products, and traditional medicines.
